roadmap.milestone: M1 — standalone DPoP-bound Solid-OIDC resource-server verifier (Rust-migration carve-out #2)
roadmap.note: A behavioural Rust port of prod-solid-server's vetted TS verifier (src/auth) — the single load-bearing security blocker of the Rust-migration spike (risk R1). Verifies RFC 9068 at+jwt access tokens + RFC 9449 DPoP proofs, asymmetric-algorithms-only, issuer-agnostic (trusted-issuer allowlist), with the three-state allow_missing_ath compat, a fail-closed pluggable jti replay store, RFC 7638 cnf.jkt proof-of-possession, and a DNS-pinned SSRF-safe WebID-resolver seam (the IP classifier + URL gate are implemented + tested in M1; the network fetch adapter is M2). KNOWN NARROWING: ES512 is rejected (jsonwebtoken cannot verify it) rather than silently accepted — a maintainer decision (spike open-decision #4). M2: the openidconnect JWKS-discovery adapter, the reqwest+hickory DNS-pinning WebID fetch, an axum shim, the 41/41 ath-patched Solid CTH run, the full 251-case auth port, and the Keycloak DPoP integration test. crates.io publish deferred — consume via git for now.
